intro: Triumfetta glabrior, commonly known as hairy trumpet vine, is a plant species native to tropical regions of Asia and Australia, characterized by its small white flowers and edible roots. The roots of Triumfetta glabrior have been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its anti-inflammatory, antipyretic, and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, alkaloids, and glycosides, which contribute to its therapeutic effects. In traditional herbal medicine, Triumfetta glabrior roots are often prepared as a decoction or infusion, and may also be used to make teas, tinctures, or capsules.
